use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
use tokio::time::{sleep, Duration};

struct Cache {
    store: Mutex<HashMap<String, String>>,
}

impl Cache {
    fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<String> {
        self.store.lock().unwrap().get(key).cloned()
    }

    fn insert(&self, key: String, value: String) {
        self.store.lock().unwrap().insert(key, value);
    }
}

async fn expensive_compute(key: &str) -> String {
    sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;
    format!("computed_{}", key)
}

async fn get_or_compute(cache: Arc<Cache>, key: String) -> String {
    if let Some(val) = cache.get(&key) {
        return val;
    }
    // BAD: concurrent requests for same key duplicate the work
    let val = expensive_compute(&key).await;
    cache.insert(key, val.clone());
    val
}
